name: Swedish Tea Ring
recipeInstructions:
1. Soften the yeast in lukewarm water.
2. Add sugar, salt, shortening, and egg to the yeast mixture. Mix thoroughly after each addition until the dough is just stiff enough to knead.
3. Turn the dough onto a lightly floured board and knead until smooth and elastic (the dough should be slightly softer than bread dough).
4. Place in a bowl, cover with a warm, damp cloth, and allow to double in bulk.
5. Work down lightly. Cover and let it double in bulk again.
6. Turn the dough onto a lightly floured board and roll into a sheet that is 1 1/2 inches thick.
7. Spread melted butter or butter substitute over the dough and sprinkle generously with sugar and cinnamon. Roll tightly into a jelly roll.
8. Pinch the edges together to join the ends, forming a ring.
9. Place in an oiled pan or on an oiled baking sheet.
10. Cut the dough into 3-inch pieces, cutting about three-quarters through the ring each time.
11. Turn each section so it lies flat on the baking sheet.
12. Sprinkle with sugar and chopped nuts.
13. Cover with a warm, damp cloth and let rise until tripled in bulk.
14. Bake in a hot oven (425°F) for about 30 minutes. If desired, add 1/2 cup raisins to the dough.
